mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off equity repricing as investors price in lower future earnings and higher default probabilities.
failure_condition: Forward returns following the trigger threshold are positive in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances, or the signal exhibits no predictive power over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation persisting alongside accommodative monetary policy erodes real yields and signals stagflation risk, which structurally depresses equity valuations and drives negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a negative predictive rank correlation over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in high-inflation/low-rate states are not statistically lower than the baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization recovery, directly boosting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in less than 45% of triggered instances over a 2-year test period, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding retail sales reflect strengthening consumer demand, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon without requiring additional macro conditions.
failure_condition: Forward returns following retail sales expansions are not significantly higher than baseline over a 3-year rolling window, or the signal exhibits a negative rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 8 percent, the Federal Reserve's aggressive tightening cycle historically compresses equity multiples and increases discount rates, leading to negative forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns in the triggered high-inflation regime are positive in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strong job growth signals resilient aggregate demand and wage-driven consumption, supporting equity valuations and driving positive forward returns over a monthly horizon despite elevated policy rates.
failure_condition: Forward returns following the job growth trigger are negative in more than 60% of instances, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect business and consumer confidence, leading to positive near-term equity returns through wealth effects and increased construction-related corporate earnings.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns in less than 45% of instances over a 2-year test period, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02.
